description: HTTP header injection in the requests library allows a remote attacker to inject arbitrary headers via a crafted URL.
NULL pointer dereference in X.509 certificate chain parsing allows a remote attacker to trigger a denial of service.
Buffer overflow in Pillow's image decoding pipeline allows arbitrary code execution via a maliciously crafted image file.
Heap buffer overflow in WebP lossless image format handling allows arbitrary code execution in affected Pillow versions.
Path traversal vulnerability in Werkzeug's static file serving allows read access to files outside the designated directory.
HTTP request smuggling in aiohttp's connection handling allows cache poisoning and request hijacking in reverse-proxy configurations.
SSRF vulnerability in urllib3 proxy handling allows requests to be forwarded to unintended internal network targets.
HTTP response splitting in Gunicorn's request parsing allows header injection via crafted chunked transfer encoding.
Denial of service via excessive CPU consumption when processing certain internationalized domain name inputs.
Denial of service in multipart form data parsing allows resource exhaustion via malformed Content-Disposition headers.
Sensitive information exposure in Ansible task output when no_log is not set for credential parameters.
SQL injection in pgvector Python client's query construction allows privilege escalation via unsanitized vector dimension parameters.
